title: Client secrets and privacy deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because Privacy minimization and absence of client secrets require threat-model and delivered-bundle review, not a small execution oracle.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Client secrets and privacy: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications misconception? An environment variable used during frontend build remains secret after bundling.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Anything delivered to browser code is public to that user; keep secrets server-side and minimize exposed personal data.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Anything delivered to browser code is public to that user; keep secrets server-side and minimize exposed personal data.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Anything delivered to browser code is public to that user; keep secrets server-side and minimize exposed personal data.
